items.t: Superhuman acquires AI detection startup GPTZero
items.d: Superhuman, which also has an AI detection tool as part of Grammarly, has snapped up GPTZero.
items.fullText: In Brief Posted: 2:48 PM PDT ┬и June 23, 2026 Image Credits: GPTZero Julie Bort Superhuman acquires AI detection startup GPTZero GPTZero, the three-year-old AI detection startup that Princeton grad Edward Tian first built as a senior thesis project, has been acquired by Superhuman, the companies announced on Tuesday. Terms of the deal were not disclosed, though Tian told Business Insider that GPTZero amassed more than 19 million registered users and $30 million in annual recurring revenue. In 2024, Tian told TechCrunch that it was profitable. Tian and co-founder CTO Alex Cui, who’d been friends since high school, raised a $3.5 million seed round led by Uncork Capital, followed by a $10 million Series A in June 2024 led by Footwork co-founder Nikhil Basu Trivedi, with several other notable investors including Reach Capital, Jack Altman’s Alt Capital, and Neo. All told, the company raised just $13.5 million. Superhuman Рђћ the company formed when Grammarly bought email provider Superhuman last year and rebranded under that name Рђћ already had an AI detection tool built into its platform. GPTZero’s mission has been to help humans detect and defend against AI slop. Grammarly’s tool has been designed to help users, often students, determine whether their writing appears AI-generated, then revise it so it doesn’t. As for why Superhuman bought a competitor, Superhuman says that “two AI detectors are better than one.” Topics GPTZero , grammarly , In Brief , Mergers and Acquisitions , Startups , superhuman , TC November 4 Boston Last chance to save up to $190 on TechCrunch Founder Summit. Join 1,000+ founders and VCs at all stages for real-world scaling insights and connections that move the needle. Savings end June 26, 11:59 p.m. PT . REGISTER NOW Newsletters See More Subscribe for the industryРђЎs biggest tech news TechCrunch Daily News Every weekday and Sunday, you can get the best of TechCrunchРђЎs coverage. items.takeaway: ВіѕьЇ╝ью┤ве╝ВЮ┤ GPTZeroвЦ╝ ВЮИВѕўьЋўВЌг AI Ж░љВДђ ЖИ░ВѕаВЮё Ж░ЋьЎћьЋеВю╝вАюВЇе AI ВЌљВЮ┤ВаёьіИВЮў ВІавб░Вё▒ВЮё вєњВЮ┤віћ вЇ░ ЖИ░ВЌгьЋа Ж▓ЃВю╝вАю в│┤ВЮИвІц.

items.t: Anthropic Customer Sues US Over Losing Access to Fable AI Model
items.d: An artificial intelligence startup that uses Anthropic PBCРђЎs AI models sued the US over a government order that the maker of Claude and Mythos keep its most advanced technology out of the hands of foreign nationals.
items.takeaway: AnthropicВЮў Ж│аЖ░ЮВЮ┤ Fable AI вфевЇИВЌљ вїђьЋю ВЋАВёИВіц ЖХїьЋюВЮё ВъЃВЮђ Ж▓ЃВю╝вАю в│┤ВЋё, AI ВЌљВЮ┤ВаёьіИВЮў ВѓгВџЕВЌљ вїђьЋю в▓ЋВаЂ вгИВаюЖ░ђ в░юВЃЮьЋа Вѕў ВъѕвІц.

items.t: After betting the firm on Anthropic, Menlo Ventures raises victorious $3B fund
items.d: Menlo has created a solid rep for itself as an AI investor, all based on one gutsy $750 million move in 2024.
items.fullText: Menlo Ventures announced $3 billion in funds on Tuesday, the largest raise in its 50-year history, driven in large part by its AI portfolio, especially Anthropic. Its stake in the model maker is now worth about $14 billion, sources told Bloomberg . To hear the folks at Menlo talk about it, they were white-knuckling it when they made a bet-the-firm $750 million investment in Anthropic in 2024, preemptively leading the model maker’s Series D. At the time, that round quadrupled the startup’s valuation to $18.4 billion . While the bet itself was arguably not wildly risky, the means by which the firm raised that kind of capital was more so. Menlo had been an early investor in the Series C, before Anthropic had a product. By 2024, long before Claude Code and Claude Mythos, the company was showing signs of success. It had landed a $4 billion deal from Amazon and was being hotly pursued by VCs, having been founded by former OpenAI researchers, including siblings CEO Dario Amodei and president Daniela Amodei. It was a rising-star AI company, as so many startups founded by OpenAI alum still are today . But how Menlo raised the funds was eye-popping. In 2024, the venture world was just rebounding from the post-pandemic VC winter, with big-money firms like SoftBank and Tiger Global still licking their wounds. No one was writing checks for three-quarters of a billion dollars. Menlo structured the bulk of deal, about $500 million worth, as a special purpose vehicle, or SPV Рђћ a one-off investment entity created to pool money from multiple sources for a single deal. Menlo also contributed $250 million from its own fund and contributions from Menlo insiders, sources told Forbes at the time , bringing the total round to $750 million. Since then, AI SPVs have become as commonplace as cockroaches, with Anthropic a particular target Рђћ so much so that the AI company issued a warning last month , calling all unauthorized SPVs and secondary markets claiming to sell its stock &#
items.takeaway: Menlo VenturesЖ░ђ AnthropicВЌљ вїђьЋю ьѕгВъљвЦ╝ Вё▒Ж│хВаЂВю╝вАю вДѕвг┤вдгьЋеВю╝вАюВЇе AI ВЌљВЮ┤ВаёьіИВЮў Ж░юв░юВЌљ вїђьЋю ьѕгВъљ ьЎЋвїђВЌљ ЖИ░ВЌгьЋа Ж▓ЃВю╝вАю в│┤ВЮИвІц.

items.t: AnthropicРђЎs Claude Tag is learning your company, one Slack message at a time
items.d: AnthropicРђЎs new Claude Tag brings an always-on AI teammate to Slack. But beyond productivity, the feature is a strategic play to capture organizational context, institutional knowledge, and enterprise workflows.
items.fullText: Anthropic is introducing a new service called Claude Tag, an Рђюalways-on ClaudeРђЮ that lives in Slack and acts as an AI teammate. The new feature Рђћ which allows users to tag @Claude to provide insights in chats and assign tasks Рђћ will be available in beta for Slack for Claude Enterprise and Claude Team customers. Claude Tag is an evolution of several integrations that already exist . Users can already DM @Claude within Slack or tag it in channels for on-demand help, and Claude Code in Slack routes coding tasks from channel mentions to full coding sessions on the web, posting updates back into the thread. But Claude Tag adds a layer of persistent context and memory that would be difficult to maintain with previous tools. РђюAs Claude follows along with its channel, it learns ever more about the work,РђЮ reads a statement from Anthropic. РђюClaude can also automatically gather facts from elsewhere in the organization, if itРђЎs granted permission to read other channels.РђЮ With Claude Tag, everyone in a given Slack channel can access a single Claude identity, meaning Рђюanyone can see what Claude has been working on, and can pick up the conversation from where the last person left off.РђЮ System administrators will specify which tools, information, and channels Claude can access, and each Claude identity will stay scoped to whichever channels the admins define, so that a Claude set up for legal work canРђЎt seed memories into the engineering channel, for example. When assigned a specific task, Claude Tag will break down the task into stages and will work through them using whichever tools it has access to, responding in a Slack thread with what it has created. But Claude Tag also features an ambient mode that proactively jumps into the chat of its own accord to keep your team updated, flag things from across the organization, and follow up on threads or tasks that have been forgotten. Anthropic says this makes it feel like youРђЎre Рђюworking with a real colleague Рђћ one that can produce wo
items.takeaway: AnthropicВЮў Claude TagЖ░ђ ЖИ░ВЌЁВЮў Slack вЕћВІюВДђвЦ╝ ьЋЎВіхьЋеВю╝вАюВЇе AI ВЌљВЮ┤ВаёьіИВЮў ЖИ░ВЌЁ вѓ┤ВЌљВёюВЮў ВаЂВџЕВЮё ьЎЋвїђьЋа Вѕў ВъѕвІц.

items.t: Fika Jobs raises $4M to build a video-first hiring platform where AI agents interview candidates
items.d: Stockholm-based startup Fika Jobs is building a video-first hiring platform that combines AI interview agents with short-form video profiles, creating something that feels like a cross between LinkedIn and TikTok.
items.fullText: The hiring process has long been criticized for its inefficiency and opacity. Candidates spend hours writing applications and submitting cover letters, only to disappear into what often feels like a black box. Generative AI has only made things messier, with employers increasingly relying on AI-powered screening systems to sift through an overwhelming number of submissions. Stockholm-based startup Fika Jobs thinks there’s a better way. The company is building a video-first hiring platform that combines AI interview agents with short-form video profiles, creating something that feels like a cross between LinkedIn and TikTok. Instead of relying solely on resumes, candidates complete AI-powered interviews designed to showcase their personality and communication skills. Fika Jobs announced on Tuesday a $4 million pre-seed round, which will be used to continue developing the platform, grow the team, and prepare for a wider launch later this year. For job seekers, the process starts by connecting a LinkedIn profile. Fika’s AI reviews the candidate’s background and generates personalized interview questions. Candidates then complete a roughly 10-minute video interview with the AI agent, currently powered by Google’s Gemini models. After the interview, Fika automatically turns responses into short video clips and organizes them into a profile. Instead of applying to every new role, candidates maintain a live profile that employers can discover and revisit as new opportunities arise. Image Credits: Fika Jobs The idea came from co-founders and brothers Jakob Dubois (CEO) and Alexander Dubois (CTO) while they were building their previous startup. РђюWhen we were building [social app] Gaff, we spent a lot of time recruiting and almost passed on a candidate because his resume did not really stand out,РђЮ Jakob Dubois told TechCrunch. РђюWe ended up speaking with him anyway, and within minutes, his grit, drive, and ambition became obvious. Exactly the kind of pe

NVIDIA Brings Trusted, 24/7 AI Agents to Telecom Operations
items.d: Telecom operators have seen remarkable returns from using generative AI to automate network management, customer care and back-office operations. Most of that impact has been taskРђЉbased: automation that speeds up predetermined steps while people manually correlate insights and direct next steps. Automation is no longer the finish line Рђћ itРђЎs the launchpad to autonomy.┬а The […]
items.fullText: NVIDIA Brings Trusted, 24/7 AI Agents to Telecom Operations At DTW Ignite 2026, NVIDIA and its partners are showcasing the data, models, simulation and secure runtime stack enabling telcos to build more secure agentic workflows across autonomous networks and operations. June 22, 2026 by Lilac Ilan 0 Comments Share Share This Article X Facebook LinkedIn Copy link Link copied! Telecom operators have seen remarkable returns from using generative AI to automate network management, customer care and back-office operations. Most of that impact has been taskРђЉbased: automation that speeds up predetermined steps while people manually correlate insights and direct next steps. Automation is no longer the finish line Рђћ itРђЎs the launchpad to autonomy. The industry is now pushing toward truly autonomous networks and operations, where AI agents proactively watch for problems and coordinate changes across network, IT and business systems. Together, synthetic data, telecom-domain models, secure agent runtimes and simulations form critical pieces of a secure, telecom autonomy platform , where agents understand operator intent, act safely across business and network domains and keep humans in control of policy. NVIDIA and its partners are demonstrating these building blocks at TM ForumРђЎs DTW Ignite 2026 Рђћ running this week in Copenhagen Рђћ giving operators a practical path to running more autonomous, resilient networks and powering richer AIРђЉdriven services for consumers and businesses. Unlock PrivacyРђЉSafe Telecom Data for AI Models Reasoning models that understand the telecom domain are the foundation of autonomous networks. These specialized models require fineРђЉtuning on highРђЉquality datasets, yet 54% of operators cite dataРђЉrelated issues as their biggest barrier, with the most valuable network and customer data too sensitive to use directly. Synthetic data is enabling operators to safely increase the volume and diversity of training data, protect sensitive information and democratiz

OpenAI launches new initiative to help find and patch open source bugs
items.d: OpenAI is using AI to help the open source community better protect itself.
items.fullText: OpenAI announced a new initiative on Monday designed to help the open source community improve its cybersecurity game and ward off bugs. “Patch the Planet” (which is a not-so-subtle allusion to “ Hack the Planet ,” the iconic catchphrase from the 1995 movie “Hackers”) will see OpenAI team up with the security company Trail of Bits to help open source maintainers secure their projects. OpenAI said security staff from Trail of Bits will work directly with open source maintainers to review potential code issues. OpenAI’s security tools Рђћ like Codex Security Рђћ will be used to assist in the process. “Many maintainers are already being asked to sort through more reports, more quickly, with the same limited time and resources,” OpenAI said Monday. “Patch the Planet is built to reduce that burden, not add to it: security engineers review findings before they reach maintainers, work with projects to develop patches and tests, and build reusable workflows that help teams continue improving security after the first fixes land.” In other words, Trail of Bits engineers will function more or less like code EMTs Рђћ there to help open source project maintainers identify and triage potential issues, all supported by OpenAI’s software. It sounds like an ambitious project, and it’s somewhat unclear how it will function in the long term, or how it plans to scale up (if at all). Open source projects are the digital bedrock upon which the commercial software industry rests, but, unfortunately, due to the decentralized and poorly monitored structure of that ecosystem, much of the software is insecure. Bugs in open source projects can turn into major problems for commercial codebases. The log4j debacle from several years ago Рђћ when a bad vulnerability was discovered in a widely used open source utility Рђћ is a good example. Much of the concern surrounding tools like Mythos (Anthropic’s highly publicized secur

items.t: [ВіцьЃђьіИВЌЁ]вЕћвћћьїћ 'ВЋаьћёьё░вІЦ'Ж│╝ 'вЕћвћћьїћ AI'вАю ВЮўвБї AI ВЌљВЮ┤ВаёьіИ ьћївъФьЈ╝ ЖхгВХЋьЋа Ж▓ЃРђЮ
The AI world is getting ‘loopy’
items.d: The loop takes agentic AI a step further by authorizing a swarm of agents to work continuously in the background, endlessly.
items.fullText: On Friday, Claude Code creator Boris Cherny made an appearance at MetaРђЎs @Scale conference and, surprisingly, the first question from the audience was about loops. РђюAre loops the next hype cycle,РђЮ the questioner asked, Рђюor are they for real?РђЮ ChernyРђЎs answer was an emphatic, РђюYes, theyРђЎre for real.РђЮ РђюTwo years ago, we wrote source code by hand. We started to transition so agents write the code. And now weРђЎre transitioning to the point where agents are prompting agents that then write the code,РђЮ he continued. РђюAs big as the step from source code to agents was, loops are just as important and as big a step.РђЮ Later in the talk (around the 32:00 mark in the YouTube video posted above), Cherny got specific about the loops he keeps running in his own work. One agent is continually looking for ways to improve the code architecture, while another looks for duplicated abstractions that can be unified. They submit pull requests like any other coder, and since the code is constantly changing, they never stop running. ItРђЎs a powerful idea, particularly with a figure as significant as Cherny behind it. With the shift to agentic AI, the focus for most users has been managing their agents as well as possible: establish clear goals, check in on discrete units of progress, and donРђЎt let them stray too far beyond the prompt. The loop takes it a step further by authorizing a swarm of agents to work continuously in the background, endlessly. ItРђЎs a lot of trust to place in AI Рђћ but with models getting better fast, it could be the next step in getting AI to handle real work. The first thing to recognize is that this isnРђЎt entirely new. Recursive loops Рђћ functions that call themselves in order to repeat an action, along with a condition that stops the loop Рђћ are a mainstay of intro computer science courses. These loops are following a non-deterministic logic Рђћ that is, it’s a subagent that chooses when to stop the loop instead of a clear condition Рђћ but the same basic approach is a

items.t: NVIDIA Vera CPU Opens the Way for Agentic Scientific AI at Los Alamos National Laboratory
items.d: Mission, Vision and Veritas Рђћ new Los Alamos National Laboratory (LANL) supercomputers to be built with HPE and NVIDIA Рђћ are tapping NVIDIA Vera CPUs to accelerate scientific discovery, unlocking agentic AI for science. The supercomputers will use the HPE Cray Supercomputing GX5000 architecture with the NVIDIA Vera Rubin platform, combining NVIDIA Vera CPUs, NVIDIA […]
items.fullText: NVIDIA Vera CPU Opens the Way for Agentic Scientific AI at Los Alamos National Laboratory Mission, Vision and Veritas supercomputers with Vera CPUs to advance materials simulation, scientific AI agents and molecular design. June 22, 2026 by Chris Porter 0 Comments Share Share This Article X Facebook LinkedIn Copy link Link copied! Mission, Vision and Veritas Рђћ new Los Alamos National Laboratory (LANL) supercomputers to be built with HPE and NVIDIA Рђћ are tapping NVIDIA Vera CPUs to accelerate scientific discovery, unlocking agentic AI for science. The supercomputers will use the HPE Cray Supercomputing GX5000 architecture with the NVIDIA Vera Rubin platform, combining NVIDIA Vera CPUs, NVIDIA Rubin GPUs and NVIDIA Quantum-X800 InfiniBand networking. Under the planned configuration, Mission will include NVIDIA Vera Rubin GPU nodes and 2,300 standalone NVIDIA Vera CPUs using the HPE Cray Supercomputing GX240 blade. Veritas will feature approximately 1,150 standalone NVIDIA Vera CPUs to complement NVIDIA Vera Rubin nodes. Veritas will arrive alongside Mission and Vision and serve the Laboratory Directed Research and Development program, helping accelerate agentic AI for science. The system will test these technologies for use in larger systems being built out at LANL. Researchers are adding a new tool for science with AI agents that can form hypotheses, choose tools, launch simulations, analyze outputs and refine the next step. LANLРђЎs public work on URSA, the Universal Research and Scientific Agent Рђћ running on Venado and soon Mission and Vision Рђћ points in this direction: a modular, feedback-driven AI framework designed to help scientists brainstorm hypotheses, plan experiments, run simulations and analyze results. LANL demonstrated that the Vera CPU delivered 7x higher performance on URSA workloads than the CPUs in the Crossroads x86 supercomputer. items.t: Eco Wave Power Turns Waves Into Watts With NVIDIA AI Infrastructure and Digital Twins
items.d: The next era of AI will not be defined by compute alone. Its growth will be determined by energy. ┬аAs accelerated computing scales across AI factories, agentic AI, industrial AI, edge computing and physical AI Рђћ including robotics and autonomous systems Рђћ global electricity demand is rising at unprecedented speed.┬а In many regions, expanding grid […]
items.fullText: Eco Wave Power Turns Waves Into Watts With NVIDIA AI Infrastructure and Digital Twins June 22, 2026 by Tenika Versey Walker 0 Comments Share Share This Article X Facebook LinkedIn Copy link Link copied! The next era of AI will not be defined by compute alone. Its growth will be determined by energy. As accelerated computing scales across AI factories , agentic AI , industrial AI , edge computing and physical AI Рђћ including robotics and autonomous systems Рђћ global electricity demand is rising at unprecedented speed. In many regions, expanding grid infrastructure to meet that need requires years of permitting, transmission upgrades, land acquisition and capital investment. This challenge is reshaping how the world thinks about energy infrastructure for AI. Eco Wave Power, a member of the NVIDIA Inception startup programРђЎs Sustainable Futures initiative , is developing technology Рђћ powered by NVIDIA AI infrastructure and digital twins Рђћ that converts energy from ocean waves into clean electricity using existing marine infrastructure. By using already-built coastal structures, wave energy generation can be deployed closer to areas with growing power demand Рђћ including ports, industrial zones and future AI infrastructure hubs. РђюWave energy is one of the largest renewable energy sources that exists,РђЮ said Inna Braverman, cofounder and CEO of Eco Wave Power. Рђю Everybody wants it, but nobody can do it, so I looked at the current problems with harnessing wave power and I asked: How do we simplify it? РђЮ Turning the Sea Into a Power Source Harnessing EarthРђЎs natural cycles for power generation isnРђЎt a new concept. Wind and solar energy have been well established industries for decades. Waves are on the way to completing this trifecta of power-producing elements. In the U.S. alone, wave energy could produce over 60% of annual energy consumption, according to the Energy Information Administration. It all starts with floaters Рђћ noninvasive floating infrastructure attached to brea

items.t: Вѓ╝Вё▒ВаёВъљ, вфевЊа ВЌЁвг┤ ЖИ░ВЌЁВџЕ В▒ЌGPT ьЎюВџЕ

AI helped diagnose 18 children whose rare diseases had stumped doctors
items.d: Researchers from Boston ChildrenРђЎs Hospital identified 18 diagnoses for children with rare diseases using OpenAIРђЎs o3 model.
items.fullText: FIRST ON NBC NEWS Artificial intelligence Artificial intelligence AI helped diagnose 18 children whose rare diseases had stumped doctors Researchers from Boston ChildrenРђЎs Hospital identified 18 diagnoses for children with rare diseases using OpenAIРђЎs o3 model. Listen to this article with a free account 00:00 00:00 Add NBC News to Google AI helped diagnose 18 children whose rare diseases had stumped doctors 03:09 Get more news Live on Share Add NBC News to Google June 18, 2026, 10:00 AM EDT By Jared Perlo Over a thousand children visit Boston ChildrenРђЎs Hospital every day. Many get clear diagnoses and begin treatment, but a small subset of pediatric visitors with rare illnesses never get diagnoses at all. That has started to change with the help of AI. Subscribe to read this story ad-free Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content. New research from the hospitalРђЎs center for rare diseases and the AI company OpenAI reveals that off-the-shelf AI tools can help identify which errors in patientsРђЎ genomes might be causing the childrenРђЎs diseases. The findings, announced Thursday in the New England Journal of MedicineРђЎs AI-focused publication, NEJM AI, show that OpenAIРђЎs o3 Deep Research model helped clarify 18 diagnoses for children who had struggled to find causes for their illnesses and symptoms. РђюItРђЎs a total game changer,РђЮ said one of the studyРђЎs lead researchers, Catherine Brownstein, the scientific director of the genetic investigations arm of the Manton Center for Orphan Disease Research at Boston ChildrenРђЎs Hospital. She said the research team analyzed several hundred genomes of patients who had not received diagnoses for their rare diseases. РђюIt got almost 5% new diagnoses, which doesnРђЎt sound like a lot,РђЮ Brownstein acknowledged, Рђюbut considering how many times these had already been analyzed, thatРђЎs a huge number, and each one means an answer for a family.РђЮ The Manton Center works with over 3,500 individuals globally and across all 50 state

France Advances EuropeРђЎs AI Future With NVIDIA Technologies
items.d: A year ago at NVIDIA GTC Paris at VivaTech, France laid out plans to advance local AI Рђћ from new AI factories and national compute capacity to open frontier models and industrial platforms. Now, that AI infrastructure is coming online. AI agents are running in production, startups are deploying applications and the French AI ecosystem […]
items.fullText: France Advances EuropeРђЎs AI Future With NVIDIA Technologies From AI factories to open models, France is turning its AI ambitions into production reality. June 17, 2026 by Nat Ives 0 Comments Share Share This Article X Facebook LinkedIn Copy link Link copied! A year ago at NVIDIA GTC Paris at VivaTech, France laid out plans to advance local AI Рђћ from new AI factories and national compute capacity to open frontier models and industrial platforms. Now, that AI infrastructure is coming online. AI agents are running in production, startups are deploying applications and the French AI ecosystem is developing models, datasets and platforms designed around local languages, cultural context and European requirements. French AI Infrastructure Takes Shape FranceРђЎs AI ambitions are gaining momentum. Billions in investment commitments through France 2030, the 2025 AI Action Summit and this yearРђЎs Choose France Summit are reinforcing the countryРђЎs position as one of EuropeРђЎs leading destinations for AI infrastructure. As part of these efforts, Mistral is building a new 44-megawatt data center in Bruy├еres-le-Ch├бtel, a commune in northern France. Announced at GTC Paris last year, MistralРђЎs first deployment is already operational with 18,000 NVIDIA GB200 systems Рђћ laying the foundation for the companyРђЎs roadmap of 200 megawatts of compute capacity across Europe by 2027. The NVIDIA Blackwell platform is designed to help AI factories maximize throughput within fixed power budgets, combining higher performanceРђЉperРђЉwatt silicon with software features that boost data center throughput in powerРђЉconstrained environments. Mistral is also working with French public investment bank Bpifrance, AI and advanced tech investment company MGX and NVIDIA to expand Campus AI , a network of AI factories anchored by a planned 1.4-gigawatt facility, making it one of EuropeРђЎs largest AI campuses. This momentum reflects a broader wave of AI infrastructure investment in France. Scaleway, a European public c
